name: codeclone
description: Structural change controller for Python — deterministic, baseline-aware, built for CI and AI agents.
long_description: CodeClone for Claude Desktop wraps the local codeclone-mcp launcher as an MCP bundle. It keeps Claude on the same canonical 23-tool MCP surface used by the CLI, HTML report, VS Code extension, and Codex plugin — read-only, baseline-aware, local stdio only. The v2.1 change controller starts with intent declaration and blast radius; patch contract, review receipt, and claim guard are planned follow-ups.

tools.name: analyze_repository
tools.description: Run a deterministic CodeClone analysis and register it as the latest MCP run.

tools.name: analyze_changed_paths
tools.description: Run a deterministic CodeClone analysis and return a changed-files projection.

tools.name: get_run_summary
tools.description: Cheapest run-level snapshot: health, findings, baseline, inventory.

tools.name: get_production_triage
tools.description: Production-first triage: health, hotspots, suggestions, source-kind counters.

tools.name: get_blast_radius
tools.description: Structural risk boundary: dependents, clone cohorts, do-not-touch paths, review context.

tools.name: manage_change_intent
tools.description: Change intent lifecycle: declare scope, get status, check diff, clear intent.

tools.name: list_hotspots
tools.description: Priority-ranked hotspot views by kind.

tools.name: list_findings
tools.description: List canonical finding groups with filters, pagination, and summary cards.

tools.name: get_finding
tools.description: Return a single canonical finding group by short or full id.

tools.name: get_remediation
tools.description: Return actionable remediation guidance for a single finding.

tools.name: get_report_section
tools.description: Return a canonical report section for the latest or specified run.

tools.name: check_clones
tools.description: Return clone findings from a compatible stored run.

tools.name: check_complexity
tools.description: Return complexity hotspots from a compatible stored run.

tools.name: check_coupling
tools.description: Return coupling hotspots from a compatible stored run.

tools.name: check_cohesion
tools.description: Return cohesion hotspots from a compatible stored run.

tools.name: check_dead_code
tools.description: Return dead-code findings from a compatible stored run.

tools.name: compare_runs
tools.description: Run-to-run delta: regressions, improvements, health change.

tools.name: evaluate_gates
tools.description: Evaluate CI gate conditions against an existing MCP run.

tools.name: generate_pr_summary
tools.description: PR-friendly markdown or JSON summary of changed files.

tools.name: mark_finding_reviewed
tools.description: Mark a finding as reviewed in the current in-memory MCP session.

tools.name: list_reviewed_findings
tools.description: List in-memory reviewed findings for the current or specified run.

tools.name: clear_session_runs
tools.description: Clear all in-memory MCP analysis runs and ephemeral session state.

tools.name: help
tools.description: Explain a CodeClone workflow or contract topic and suggest the safest next step.

workspace_root.title: Workspace root path
workspace_root.description: Optional absolute path to the Python project root (the directory that contains .venv or venv). When set, the launcher prefers that virtual environment over user-local or system-wide installs. Useful when Claude Desktop starts outside the project directory.

launcher_command.title: CodeClone launcher command
launcher_command.description: Optional absolute path or bare command name for codeclone-mcp. Leave empty to prefer workspace launchers, then user-local paths, then PATH.

launcher_args_json.title: Advanced launcher args (JSON array)
launcher_args_json.description: Optional JSON array of additional codeclone-mcp arguments. Transport is always stdio and cannot be overridden.
